initial_program:
% Output grid is 3x3
row(0).
row(1).
row(2).
col(0).
col(1).
col(2).
output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% Exactly one color for each output cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Define the order (Pos) of each nonzero input cell in columnmajor order.
% For a cell (R,C,V) the order is the number of nonzero cells
% in earlier columns plus the number of cells in the same column
% with a smaller row index.
pos(R,C,Pos) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 0,
    Pos = #count{ R2,C2,V2 : input(R2,C2,V2), V2 != 0, C2 < C } +
          #count{ R2 : input(R2,C,V2), V2 != 0, R2 < R }.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Map an output cell to the input cell whose order matches its position.
output(R_out,C_out,V) :-
    input(R_in,C_in,V),
    pos(R_in,C_in,Pos),
    (R_out * 3) + C_out = Pos.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Existence of a matching input cell for a given output cell and value.
exists(R_out,C_out,V) :- input(R_in,C_in,V), pos(R_in,C_in,Pos), (R_out * 3) + C_out = Pos.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Constraint: a nonzero color is only allowed when a matching input cell exists.
:- not exists(R_out,C_out,V), V != 0.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% The required output.
#show output/3.

rounds.program_after:
% Output grid is 3x3
row(0).
row(1).
row(2).
col(0).
col(1).
col(2).
output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% Exactly one color for each output cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Define the order (Pos) of each nonzero input cell in columnmajor order.
% For a cell (R,C,V) the order is the number of nonzero cells
% in earlier columns plus the number of cells in the same column
% with a smaller row index.
pos(R,C,Pos) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 0,
    Pos = #count{ R2,C2,V2 : input(R2,C2,V2), V2 != 0, C2 < C } +
          #count{ R2 : input(R2,C,V2), V2 != 0, R2 < R }.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Map an output cell to the input cell whose order matches its position.
output(R_out,C_out,V) :-
    input(R_in,C_in,V),
    pos(R_in,C_in,Pos),
    (R_out * 3) + C_out = Pos.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Existence of a matching input cell for a given output cell and value.
exists(R_out,C_out,V) :- input(R_in,C_in,V), pos(R_in,C_in,Pos), (R_out * 3) + C_out = Pos.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Constraint: a nonzero color is only allowed when a matching input cell exists.
:- not exists(R_out,C_out,V), V != 0.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% The required output.
#show output/3.

rounds.program_after:
% Output grid is 3x3
row(0).
row(1).
row(2).
col(0).
col(1).
col(2).
output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% Exactly one color for each output cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Define the order (Pos) of each nonzero input cell in columnmajor order.
% For a cell (R,C,V) the order is the number of nonzero cells
% in earlier columns plus the number of cells in the same column
% with a smaller row index.
pos(R,C,Pos) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 0,
    cnt_before(R,C,N1),
    cnt_samecol(R,C,N2),
    Pos = N1 + N2.

cnt_before(R,C,N1) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0, N1 = #count{R2,C2,V2 : input(R2,C2,V2), V2 != 0, C2 < C }.

cnt_samecol(R,C,N2) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0, N2 = #count{R2 : input(R2,C,V2), V2 != 0, R2 < R }.


% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Map an output cell to the input cell whose order matches its position.
output(R_out,C_out,V) :-
    input(R_in,C_in,V),
    pos(R_in,C_in,Pos),
    (R_out * 3) + C_out = Pos.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Existence of a matching input cell for a given output cell and value.
exists(R_out,C_out,V) :- input(R_in,C_in,V), pos(R_in,C_in,Pos), (R_out * 3) + C_out = Pos.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Constraint: a nonzero color is only allowed when a matching input cell exists.
:- not exists(R_out,C_out,V), V != 0.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% The required output.
#show output/3.

26: cnt_samecol(R,C,N2) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0, N2 = #count{R2 : input(R2,C,V2), V2 != 0, R2 < R }.
27: 
28: 
29: % ------------------------------------------------------------
30: % Map an output cell to the input cell whose order matches its position.
31: output(R_out,C_out,V) :-
32:     input(R_in,C_in,V),
33:     pos(R_in,C_in,Pos),
34:     (R_out * 3) + C_out = Pos.
35: 
36: % ------------------------------------------------------------
37: % Existence of a matching input cell for a given output cell and value.
38: exists(R_out,C_out,V) :- input(R_in,C_in,V), pos(R_in,C_in,Pos), (R_out * 3) + C_out = Pos.
39: 
40: % ------------------------------------------------------------
41: % Constraint: a nonzero color is only allowed when a matching input cell exists.
42: :- not exists(R_out,C_out,V), V != 0.
43: 
44: % ------------------------------------------------------------
45: % The required output.
46: #show output/3.

rounds.program_after:
% Output grid is 3x3
row(0).
row(1).
row(2).
col(0).
col(1).
col(2).
output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% Exactly one color for each output cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Define the order (Pos) of each nonzero input cell in columnmajor order.
% For a cell (R,C,V) the order is the number of nonzero cells
% in earlier columns plus the number of cells in the same column
% with a smaller row index.
pos(R,C,Pos) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 0,
    cnt_before(R,C,N1),
    cnt_samecol(R,C,N2),
    Pos = N1 + N2.

cnt_before(R,C,N1) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0, N1 = #count{R2,C2,V2 : input(R2,C2,V2), V2 != 0, C2 < C }.

cnt_samecol(R,C,N2) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0, N2 = #count{R2 : input(R2,C,V2), V2 != 0, R2 < R }.


% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Map an output cell to the input cell whose order matches its position.
output(R_out,C_out,V) :-
    input(R_in,C_in,V),
    pos(R_in,C_in,Pos),
    (R_out * 3) + C_out = Pos.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Existence of a matching input cell for a given output cell and value.
exists(R_out,C_out,V) :- input(R_in,C_in,V), pos(R_in,C_in,Pos), (R_out * 3) + C_out = Pos.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Constraint: a nonzero color is only allowed when a matching input cell exists.
:- not exists(R_out,C_out,V), V != 0.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% The required output.
#show output/3.

final_program:
% Output grid is 3x3
row(0).
row(1).
row(2).
col(0).
col(1).
col(2).
output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% Exactly one color for each output cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Define the order (Pos) of each nonzero input cell in columnmajor order.
% For a cell (R,C,V) the order is the number of nonzero cells
% in earlier columns plus the number of cells in the same column
% with a smaller row index.
pos(R,C,Pos) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 0,
    cnt_before(R,C,N1),
    cnt_samecol(R,C,N2),
    Pos = N1 + N2.

cnt_before(R,C,N1) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0, N1 = #count{R2,C2,V2 : input(R2,C2,V2), V2 != 0, C2 < C }.

cnt_samecol(R,C,N2) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0, N2 = #count{R2 : input(R2,C,V2), V2 != 0, R2 < R }.


% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Map an output cell to the input cell whose order matches its position.
output(R_out,C_out,V) :-
    input(R_in,C_in,V),
    pos(R_in,C_in,Pos),
    (R_out * 3) + C_out = Pos.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Existence of a matching input cell for a given output cell and value.
exists(R_out,C_out,V) :- input(R_in,C_in,V), pos(R_in,C_in,Pos), (R_out * 3) + C_out = Pos.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Constraint: a nonzero color is only allowed when a matching input cell exists.
:- not exists(R_out,C_out,V), V != 0.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% The required output.
#show output/3.
